title: LabelRoman
lean_code_with_comments:
def Walk.replaceWalkCaseI {G : CDMG Node} :
    ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, 0 => True
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ + 1 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.forwardE _) _, 0 => True
  | _, _, .cons _ (.backwardE _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, j + 1 => p.replaceWalkCaseI j

abbrev G : CDMG Bool where
  J := ‚àÖ
  V := Finset.univ
  hJV_disj := by simp
  E := {(false, true), (true, false), (true, true)}
  hE_subset := by
    intro e he
    fin_cases he <;> exact ‚ü®by decide, by decide‚ü©
  L := ‚àÖ
  hL_subset := by intro _ hs; cases hs
  hL_irrefl := by intro _ hs; cases hs

def œÄ : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.backwardE h_ba)
    (Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_bb)
      (Walk.nil true h_b_mem))

def œÉ_ij : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_ab) (Walk.nil true h_b_mem)

def œÄ' : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_ab)
    (Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_bb)
      (Walk.nil true h_b_mem))

theorem not_replace_walk :
    ‚àÉ (Node : Type) (_inst : DecidableEq Node) (G : CDMG Node)
      (C : Set Node) (hC : C ‚äÜ ‚ÜëG.J ‚à™ ‚ÜëG.V) (u w : Node)
      (œÄ : Walk G u w) (hœÄ : œÄ.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC)
      (i j : ‚Ñï) (hij : i < j) (hjn : j ‚â§ œÄ.length)
      (v_i v_j : Node)
      (h_get_i : œÄ.vertices[i]? = some v_i)
      (h_get_j : œÄ.vertices[j]? = some v_j)
      (h_Sc : v_i ‚àà G.Sc v_j)
      (œÉ_ij : Walk G v_i v_j) (œÄ' : Walk G u w),
      (œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j ‚Üí
         œÉ_ij.IsDirectedWalk ‚àß
         (‚àÄ œÑ : Walk G v_i v_j, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ œÑ.length)) ‚àß
      (¬¨ œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j ‚Üí
         œÉ_ij.reverse.IsDirectedWalk ‚àß
         (‚àÄ œÑ : Walk G v_j v_i, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.reverse.length ‚â§ œÑ.length)) ‚àß
      (‚àÄ x ‚àà œÉ_ij.vertices, x ‚àà G.Sc v_j) ‚àß
      œÄ'.vertices = (œÄ.vertices.take (i + 1)).dropLast ++ œÉ_ij.vertices ++
          œÄ.vertices.drop (j + 1) ‚àß
      ¬¨ œÄ'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC
-- claim_3_27 -- end statement
  := by
    refine ‚ü®Bool, inferInstance, G, ‚àÖ, Set.empty_subset _, false, true, œÄ,
            œÄ_isSigmaOpen (Set.empty_subset _), 0, 1, Nat.zero_lt_one,
            (by decide : 1 ‚â§ œÄ.length), false, true, rfl, rfl, Sc_a_b,
            œÉ_ij, œÄ',
            ?hCaseI,
            (fun hno => absurd œÄ_replaceWalkCaseI_one hno),
            ?hP3, rfl, ?hP5‚ü©
    case hCaseI =>
      -- Case (i) fires (`œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI 1` holds via the
      -- `.forwardE h_bb` self-loop step at position 1 on `œÄ`).  The
      -- conjunction has two parts:
      --   (a) `œÉ_ij.IsDirectedWalk`              ‚Äî `œÉ_ij_isDirected`;
      --   (b) `‚àÄ œÑ, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ œÑ.length`
      --                                          ‚Äî discharged below.
      -- For (b): `œÉ_ij.length = 1` (a single `.forwardE` cons cell),
      -- so we need `1 ‚â§ œÑ.length` for any directed walk
      -- `œÑ : Walk G false true`.  A length-0 walk would be `Walk.nil
      -- v hv` of type `Walk G v v`, which would force `v = false` and
      -- `v = true` ‚Äî impossible by `Bool` constructor injectivity.
      -- Lean's index unifier eliminates the `.nil` branch of the
      -- `match` automatically; only the `.cons _ _ p` branch survives,
      -- and its length is `p.length + 1 ‚â• 1`.
      intro _
      refine ‚ü®œÉ_ij_isDirected, ?_‚ü©
      intro œÑ _hœÑ
      match œÑ with
      | .cons _ _ p =>
          change œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ p.length + 1
          change (1 : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ p.length + 1
          exact Nat.succ_le_succ (Nat.zero_le _)
    case hP3 =>
      intro x hx
      -- œÉ_ij.vertices = [false, true]; split on x = false vs x = true.
      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx2
      ¬∑ exact Sc_a_b
      ¬∑ rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx2 with rfl | hx3
        ¬∑ exact Sc_b_b
        ¬∑ cases hx3
    case hP5 =>
      intro hopen
      obtain ‚ü®hcol, _‚ü© := hopen
      have hin : true ‚àà G.AncSet (‚àÖ : Set Bool) :=
        hcol 1 true œÄ'_vertices_one œÄ'_isCollider_one
      -- `G.AncSet ‚àÖ = ‚ãÉ v ‚àà ‚àÖ, G.Anc v = ‚àÖ`, so `true ‚àà ‚àÖ` is False.
      simp [CDMG.AncSet] at hin

def Walk.replaceWalkCaseI {G : CDMG Node} :
    ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, 0 => True
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ + 1 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.forwardE _) _, 0 => True
  | _, _, .cons _ (.backwardE _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, j + 1 => p.replaceWalkCaseI j

abbrev G : CDMG Bool where
  J := ‚àÖ
  V := Finset.univ
  hJV_disj := by simp
  E := {(false, true), (true, false), (true, true)}
  hE_subset := by
    intro e he
    fin_cases he <;> exact ‚ü®by decide, by decide‚ü©
  L := ‚àÖ
  hL_subset := by intro _ hs; cases hs
  hL_irrefl := by intro _ hs; cases hs

def œÄ : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.backwardE h_ba)
    (Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_bb)
      (Walk.nil true h_b_mem))

def œÉ_ij : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_ab) (Walk.nil true h_b_mem)

def œÄ' : Walk G false true :=
  Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_ab)
    (Walk.cons true (WalkStep.forwardE h_bb)
      (Walk.nil true h_b_mem))

theorem not_replace_walk :
    ‚àÉ (Node : Type) (_inst : DecidableEq Node) (G : CDMG Node)
      (C : Set Node) (hC : C ‚äÜ ‚ÜëG.J ‚à™ ‚ÜëG.V) (u w : Node)
      (œÄ : Walk G u w) (hœÄ : œÄ.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC)
      (i j : ‚Ñï) (hij : i < j) (hjn : j ‚â§ œÄ.length)
      (v_i v_j : Node)
      (h_get_i : œÄ.vertices[i]? = some v_i)
      (h_get_j : œÄ.vertices[j]? = some v_j)
      (h_Sc : v_i ‚àà G.Sc v_j)
      (œÉ_ij : Walk G v_i v_j) (œÄ' : Walk G u w),
      (œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j ‚Üí
         œÉ_ij.IsDirectedWalk ‚àß
         (‚àÄ œÑ : Walk G v_i v_j, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ œÑ.length)) ‚àß
      (¬¨ œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j ‚Üí
         œÉ_ij.reverse.IsDirectedWalk ‚àß
         (‚àÄ œÑ : Walk G v_j v_i, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.reverse.length ‚â§ œÑ.length)) ‚àß
      (‚àÄ x ‚àà œÉ_ij.vertices, x ‚àà G.Sc v_j) ‚àß
      œÄ'.vertices = (œÄ.vertices.take (i + 1)).dropLast ++ œÉ_ij.vertices ++
          œÄ.vertices.drop (j + 1) ‚àß
      ¬¨ œÄ'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC

  := by
    refine ‚ü®Bool, inferInstance, G, ‚àÖ, Set.empty_subset _, false, true, œÄ,
            œÄ_isSigmaOpen (Set.empty_subset _), 0, 1, Nat.zero_lt_one,
            (by decide : 1 ‚â§ œÄ.length), false, true, rfl, rfl, Sc_a_b,
            œÉ_ij, œÄ',
            ?hCaseI,
            (fun hno => absurd œÄ_replaceWalkCaseI_one hno),
            ?hP3, rfl, ?hP5‚ü©
    case hCaseI =>

      intro _
      refine ‚ü®œÉ_ij_isDirected, ?_‚ü©
      intro œÑ _hœÑ
      match œÑ with
      | .cons _ _ p =>
          change œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ p.length + 1
          change (1 : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ p.length + 1
          exact Nat.succ_le_succ (Nat.zero_le _)
    case hP3 =>
      intro x hx

      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx2
      ¬∑ exact Sc_a_b
      ¬∑ rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx2 with rfl | hx3
        ¬∑ exact Sc_b_b
        ¬∑ cases hx3
    case hP5 =>
      intro hopen
      obtain ‚ü®hcol, _‚ü© := hopen
      have hin : true ‚àà G.AncSet (‚àÖ : Set Bool) :=
        hcol 1 true œÄ'_vertices_one œÄ'_isCollider_one

      simp [CDMG.AncSet] at hin
lean_explanation:
This row is a **disproof** of LN's `lem:replace_walk`. The theorem `not_replace_walk` is an explicit-witness existential: it packs every binder of the prove-side claim together with concrete witnesses on which the LN's conclusion (2) fails. The witness positions line up one-for-one with the prove-side's parameter list.

The existential binders correspond to the LN's data as follows. `Node : Type` is the vertex carrier (instantiated to `Bool` in the proof); `DecidableEq Node` is needed so that `Finset`-valued projections of the graph compute. `G : CDMG Node` is the graph; `C : Set Node` is the conditioning set with `hC : C ‚äÜ ‚ÜëG.J ‚à™ ‚ÜëG.V` typing it inside the input ‚à™ vertex set (the `‚Üë` coercions land the `Finset`-valued `G.J`, `G.V` into `Set Node`). `u w : Node` are the walk endpoints and `œÄ : Walk G u w` the walk itself, with `hœÄ : œÄ.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC` witnessing $C$-œÉ-openness. Positions `i j : ‚Ñï` with `hij : i < j` and `hjn : j ‚â§ œÄ.length` pick the replacement window; `v_i v_j : Node` are the vertices at those positions, certified by `h_get_i / h_get_j` against `œÄ.vertices[i]? = some v_i` etc.; `h_Sc : v_i ‚àà G.Sc v_j` is the LN's SCC hypothesis. Finally `œÉ_ij : Walk G v_i v_j` is the LN's prescribed replacement subwalk and `œÄ' : Walk G u w` the modified walk.

The conjunction body asserts five facts: a case-(i)/case-(ii) implication pair giving direction (and **minimality**) of `œÉ_ij` over directed walks $v_i \to v_j$ (or $v_j \to v_i$); $\sigma_{ij}.\text{vertices} \subseteq G.\text{Sc}\,v_j$; the LN's vertex-list factorisation $\pi'.\text{vertices} = (\pi.\text{vertices}.\text{take}(i+1)).\text{dropLast} \;\verb|++|\; \sigma_{ij}.\text{vertices} \;\verb|++|\; \pi.\text{vertices}.\text{drop}(j+1)$; and the punchline `¬¨ œÄ'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC` ‚Äî the LN's claim that $\pi'$ remains $\sigma$-open is refuted on this instance.

The helper predicate `Walk.replaceWalkCaseI œÄ j` is a structural-recursion `Prop` on `(œÄ, j)` capturing the LN's case-(i)/case-(ii) discriminant: it returns `True` precisely when `j = œÄ.length` or the walk-step at position $j$ is a `.forwardE` (a directed E-edge $v_j \to v_{j+1}$), and `False` for `.backwardE` and `.bidir`. Mathlib idioms worth flagging: `Walk G u v` and `WalkStep G u v` are inductive families indexed by start/end vertices (cons-cells store a `WalkStep G u mid` and a `Walk G mid v`); `WalkStep` has three constructors `.forwardE / .backwardE / .bidir` for the three edge types; `Walk.IsCollider œÄ k` reduces by structural recursion to a conjunction `s‚ÇÄ.IsInto v_k ‚àß s‚ÇÅ.IsInto v_k` of arrowhead-into-vertex predicates on the two walk-steps incident to position $k$; `G.Sc v` denotes the strongly-connected-component set defined as `G.Anc v ‚à© G.Desc v`.
design_choices:
**Existential-witness counter-example shape.** Two negation shapes were considered. The flat `¬¨ (‚àÄ ‚Ä¶ ‚àÉ ‚Ä¶)` shape instantiates as nested `intro`s over each of the 16 prove-side binders, with tactic bookkeeping scaling linearly with the binder count. The explicit-witness shape, used here, packs every witness into a single `‚àÉ` and discharges each conjunct of the negated body inline. We chose the explicit-witness shape because the prove-side `replace_walk` already uses an explicit parameter list (not `‚àÄ ...` in the conclusion), so witness positions in `not_replace_walk` mirror the prove-side's parameter positions one-for-one ‚Äî a reader can trace each witness back to a prove-side binder by counting.

**Minimality clauses on `œÉ_ij` alongside direction.** The LN prescribes $\sigma_{ij}$ as a *shortest* directed walk from $v_i$ to $v_j$ (case (i)) or as the reverse of a shortest directed walk from $v_j$ to $v_i$ (case (ii)). Without an explicit minimality clause the Lean negation would be strictly weaker than $\neg$(LN claim): a non-shortest $\sigma_{ij}$ in $\text{Sc}(v_j)$ might fail $\sigma$-openness on its $\pi'$ splice even when the LN's shortest does not. Adding `‚àÄ œÑ : Walk G v_i v_j, œÑ.IsDirectedWalk ‚Üí œÉ_ij.length ‚â§ œÑ.length` (and its reverse on case (ii)) pins length and restores one-for-one correspondence with the LN's prose.

**Dedicated helper predicate `Walk.replaceWalkCaseI`, not an inline disjunction.** Encoding the case discriminant inline would force two near-duplicate disjunction-of-WalkStep-constructor patterns into the binder block. Pulling out a named helper consolidates the discriminant into a single `Prop` on `(œÄ, j)`, makes the signature readable, and gives the proof phase a clean `(h : œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j)` / `(h : ¬¨ œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI j)` hypothesis to case-split on. `.bidir` is excluded from case (i) because the LN's `\tuh` macro encodes a directed E-edge with tail at $v_j$ and head at $v_{j+1}$, whereas a `.bidir` step encodes a bidirected $L$-edge placing arrowheads at both endpoints (the LN's `\huh`).

**Counter-example data over `Bool`.** Two nodes is the minimum cardinality of $V$: the hypothesis $i < j$ plus $v_i \in \text{Sc}^G(v_j)$ forces a non-trivial SCC component. The directed 2-cycle $\{(a,b), (b,a)\}$ establishes $\text{Sc}^G(b) = \{a, b\}$; the directed self-loop $(b, b)$ is the load-bearing third edge ‚Äî the syntactic source of the new collider on $\pi'$ that breaks œÉ-openness, without affecting the SCC computation. $L := \emptyset$ keeps `WalkStep.bidir` out of scope, isolating the failure to the directed-edge encoding. Using `Bool` (vs an abstract `Fin 2`) makes every `CDMG` field reduce decidably, so `decide` / `rfl` / `trivial` discharge the structural fields. `abbrev` rather than `def` for `G` makes structure projections reduce definitionally.

**Case (i) is the active failure trigger.** A single concrete failing instance refutes the universally-quantified claim, so building both case-(i) and case-(ii) counter-examples is unnecessary. Case (i) is the cleaner direction: the LN's "shortest directed path $v_i \to \dots \to v_j$" lines up with `WalkStep.forwardE`'s default orientation, and the case-(ii) implication discharges vacuously since `œÄ.replaceWalkCaseI 1` holds. The collider mechanism on $\pi'$ is directly readable in the Lean term as `(.forwardE h_ab).IsInto true ‚àß (.forwardE h_bb).IsInto true`, both conjuncts firing by node-equality on `WalkStep`'s type indices. A sibling file `Section3_3/LabelRoman.lean` retains the positive theorem signature with a `:= by sorry` placeholder, intentionally left intact in case a future remediation tightens case (i)'s trigger to exclude $v_{j+1} = v_j$ and routes self-loops into case (ii) ‚Äî in which event this disproof file remains as historical documentation.
